changes_made: Renamed k8s_cluster_automated_backup_enabled to k8s_cluster_backup_automation_enabled for consistency
review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they focus on backup and recovery, which are crucial for system recovery and reconstitution. The naming conventions mostly follow the pattern but needed slight adjustments for clarity and consistency. The functions map well to ensuring that critical data and configurations are recoverable in Kubernetes.
naming_improvements: Use 'backup_automation_enabled' for consistency with existing naming patterns
title: System Recovery And Reconstitution - System recovery and reconstitution | transaction recovery implement transaction
step1_reasoning: The control CP-10(2) focuses on system recovery and transaction recovery, which in a Kubernetes context can be mapped to ensuring that critical data such as etcd (which stores cluster state) is backed up and that there are policies in place for recovering workloads and configurations. While Kubernetes does not handle transaction recovery in the same way as a database, ensuring etcd backups and having disaster recovery policies for pods and cluster configurations can help achieve similar objectives.
